ddls:
CREATE TABLE "clothing_items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each clothing item */,
  "sku" TEXT /* Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) for the item */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the item */,
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the brand of the item */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the item */,
  "material" TEXT /* Material used for the item */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the item */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the item (e.g., Men, Women, Unisex) */,
  "size_chart_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the size chart specific to the brand and category */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* Date when the item was added to the inventory */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Date when the item details were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clothing_items_brand_id FOREIGN KEY ("brand_id") REFERENCES brands ("brand_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_clothing_items_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "size_specifications" (
  "spec_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each size specification */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the clothing item */,
  "tag_size" TEXT /* Tag size (e.g., S, M, L) */,
  "bust" REAL /* Bust measurement in inches */,
  "sleeve_length" REAL /* Sleeve length measurement in inches */,
  "shoulder" REAL /* Shoulder width measurement in inches */,
  "length" REAL /* Overall length measurement in inches */,
  "waist" REAL /* Waist measurement in inches */,
  "hip" REAL /* Hip measurement in inches */,
  "chest" REAL /* Chest measurement in inches */,
  "inseam" REAL /* Inseam measurement in inches */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* Date when the size specification was added */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Date when the size specification details were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("spec_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_size_specifications_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES clothing_items ("item_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Tops, Bottoms) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender associated with the category (e.g., Men, Women, Unisex) */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* Date when the category was added */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Date when the category details were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "brands" (
  "brand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brand */,
  "brand_name" TEXT /* Name of the brand */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the brand */,
  "date_added" TEXT /* Date when the brand was added */,
  "date_updated" TEXT /* Date when the brand details were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("brand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., inventory manager, quality control, customer service) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the user joined */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "item_id" INTEGER /* ID of the clothing item being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the item */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the item was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "device" TEXT /* Device used for access (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for the device and browser */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Details about the action performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_item_id FOREIGN KEY ("item_id") REFERENCES clothing_items ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
